intro: Trisetopsis capensis, also known as Devil's Claw, is a perennial plant native to southern Africa, belonging to the family Pedaliaceae. Traditionally, Trisetopsis capensis has been used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including relief from pain, inflammation, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include harpagoside, a sesquiterpene glycoside, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. In herbal medicine, Trisetopsis capensis is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tablet, and is commonly used to treat arthritis, fibromyalgia, and other chronic pain conditions.
